free_text: Nuclear structure of 127Te studied with (n,gamma)
and (d,p) reactions and interpreted with IBFM and QPM
The target was made from metallic tellurium enriched
to 98% (126Te) deposited in vacuum on a carbon foil
backing (about 5 mu-g/cm2).
The reaction products were analyzed with the
Munich Q3D spectrograph, and then detected in a 1.8 m
long focal plane position sensitive prop. counter.
For each angle two runs were measured, with spin 'up'
and 'down', respectively. For each peak in the
spectrum, and at each angle, both the differential
cross section and the analyzing power were determined.
Experimental error
Error in the weighted average values of the
outgoing energy over the all angle measurements.
Weighted average value of the outgoing energy
over the all angle measurements.
The E-EXC values indicate only rough positions of the
observed peaks which were obtained on the earlier
stage of this study. Historically the aim of this
preliminary fit was to localize the L=1 transitions
from the angular distributions with the purpose to
extract their centroids for the final accurate energy
calibration using the corresponding (n,g) energies.
Results from (n,gamma) reaction are
included in Entry 31546
Data received from Dr. V.Bondarenko
Entry approved by Dr. V.Bondarenko
